title: Desktop Phase 1 — Remaining Three Features
consumer_purpose: Deliver three independent Phase 1 features: Agent Configuration UI, Context Display/Overflow Awareness, and Settings Polish. All changes renderer-only in packages/app/src/.
content:
# Desktop Phase 1 — Remaining Three Features ## Architecture Overview All three features target `packages/app/src/` — the renderer/shared package. No server-side SDK changes, no Electron main process changes. Changes are additive to existing patterns: Dialog + List for management UIs, Dialog with form fields for editing, CSS variable override for color thresholds, and localized state for search/filter. ### Storage Pattern (corrected) Custom agents use **direct localStorage** with key `opencode-custom-agents-v1`, wrapped in `loadAgents()` / `saveAgents()` utilities. This works in both web and desktop contexts, requires no IPC or new context providers. Pattern: ```typescript const AGENTS_KEY = "opencode-custom-agents-v1" function loadAgents(): LocalAgent[] { try { return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(AGENTS_KEY) ?? "[]") } catch { return [] } } function saveAgents(agents: LocalAgent[]) { localStorage.setItem(AGENTS_KEY, JSON.stringify(agents)) } ``` --- ## Feature 1: Agent Configuration UI ### Data Flow ``` User opens Settings → Agents tab → "Manage" → DialogManageAgents ├── mount → loadAgents() from localStorage + local.agent.list() from SDK ├── Merge in createMemo → sorted grouped List │ ├── "Custom" group: name + badge + edit/delete │ └── "Built-in" group: name + badge (read-only) ├── "Create agent" → DialogEditAgent{mode:"create"} │ └── Save → saveAgents([...agents, newAgent]) └── Edit → DialogEditAgent{mode:"edit",agent} → saveAgents(updatedList) ``` ### Files to Create **1. packages/app/src/components/dialog-manage-agents.tsx** (~170 lines) - Pattern: Dialog + List from dialog-manage-models.tsx - Imports: `Dialog`, `List`, `Button`, `useLocal`, `useLanguage`, `useDialog`, `onMount`, `createSignal`, `createMemo` - Structure: - `Dialog` with title, description, action (plus button for create) - `List` with search, emptyMessage, filterKeys, sortBy, groupBy source - Custom agent items: name + "Custom" badge + edit button + delete button - SDK agent items: name + "Built-in" badge (no action) - Delete confirmation via lightweight inline confirm - Persistence helpers inline (`loadAgents`, `saveAgents`, `LocalAgent` type) **2. packages/app/src/components/dialog-edit-agent.tsx** (~220 lines) - Pattern: Dialog with form fields - Props: `{ agent?: LocalAgent; onSave: (a: LocalAgent) => void; onCancel: () => void }` - Fields: - `TextField` for name (required) - `TextField` for description (optional) - `TextField` (multiline) for system prompt - `Select` for model (populated from `local.model.list()`) - `TextField` number for temperature (0-2, step 0.1) - `TextField` number for max steps (default 0 = unlimited) - Actions: Save (validated) + Cancel **3. packages/app/src/components/settings-agents.tsx** (~35 lines) - Pattern: settings tab panel (like SettingsModels) - Shows agent count + "Manage" button that opens DialogManageAgents via useDialog() ### Files to Modify **4. packages/app/src/components/dialog-settings.tsx** (+15 lines) - Add `import { SettingsAgents } from "./settings-agents"` after SettingsModels - Add Tabs.Trigger for "agents" between "shortcuts" and "providers" (under a new section or in desktop section) - Add Tabs.Content for "agents" between shortcuts and providers content - Add to the search tab definitions for Feature 3 integration **5. packages/app/src/i18n/en.ts** (+18 keys) ``` dialog.agent.manage, dialog.agent.manage.description, dialog.agent.search.placeholder, dialog.agent.empty, dialog.agent.create, dialog.agent.edit, dialog.agent.fields.name, dialog.agent.fields.description, dialog.agent.fields.prompt, dialog.agent.fields.model, dialog.agent.fields.temperature, dialog.agent.fields.temperature.description, dialog.agent.fields.steps, dialog.agent.action.save, dialog.agent.action.cancel, dialog.agent.action.delete, dialog.agent.custom.badge, dialog.agent.sdk.badge ``` **6. All other i18n/*.ts files** — add same keys with English fallback ### Dirty File Awareness - `dialog-settings.tsx` is clean (check git status) - `dialog-select-mcp.tsx` is dirty from another lane — not touched --- ## Feature 2: Context Display / Overflow Awareness ### Current State (shipping) - `SessionContextUsage` renders `ProgressCircle` with percentage from `getSessionContextMetrics()` - Imported in `message-timeline.tsx` (line 1404) and `session-side-panel.tsx` (line 280) - Compaction `TurnDivider` with `label:"compaction"` already renders in timeline ### Changes **1. packages/app/src/components/session-context-usage.tsx** (+15 lines) **A) Color threshold on ProgressCircle:** ```tsx // Derive usage percentage (memoized) const usage = createMemo(() => context()?.usage ?? 0) // Color threshold CSS override — CSS variables cascade to SVG children const circle = () => (
= 80 ? "var(--state-fg-danger)" // Red ≥ 80% : usage() >= 60 ? "var(--state-fg-warning)" // Yellow 60-79% : undefined, // Default color < 60% } as JSX.CSSProperties} >
) ``` Why this works: `[data-slot="progress-circle-progress"] { stroke: var(--border-active); }` The wrapper div overrides `--border-active` which cascades to the SVG child circle's stroke. No changes to UI package needed. **B) Compaction count badge:** ```tsx // Count compaction parts from sync data const compactionCount = createMemo(() => { const msgs = params.id ? (sync.data.message[params.id] ?? []) : [] return msgs.filter(msg => (sync.data.part[msg.id] ?? []).some(p => p.type === "compaction") ).length }) // In the circle wrapper, add badge when count > 0 const circle = () => (
= 80 ? "var(--state-fg-danger)" : usage() >= 60 ? "var(--state-fg-warning)" : undefined, } as JSX.CSSProperties} > 0}> {compactionCount()}
) ``` **C) Also add `JSX` to the import** from `solid-js` for the `JSX.CSSProperties` cast. ### Files Modified - `packages/app/src/components/session-context-usage.tsx` ### Design Notes - Thresholds: <60% default, 60-79% warning (yellow), ≥80% danger (red) - No IPC, no new events, no server-side changes - Compaction count uses existing `sync.data.part` structure — no new data flow --- ## Feature 3: Settings Polish ### Changes **1. packages/app/src/components/dialog-settings.tsx** (+50 lines) Full revised component: ```tsx import { Component, createMemo, createSignal, Show } from "solid-js" import { Dialog } from "@opencode-ai/ui/dialog" import { Tabs } from "@opencode-ai/ui/tabs" import { TextField } from "@opencode-ai/ui/text-field" // NEW import import { Icon } from "@opencode-ai/ui/icon" import { useLanguage } from "@/context/language" import { usePlatform } from "@/context/platform" import { SettingsGeneral } from "./settings-general" import { SettingsKeybinds } from "./settings-keybinds" import { SettingsAgents } from "./settings-agents" // NEW import import { SettingsProviders } from "./settings-providers" import { SettingsModels } from "./settings-models" export const DialogSettings: Component = () => { const language = useLanguage() const platform = usePlatform() // ── Search/filter ── const [search, setSearch] = createSignal("") const TABS = [ { value: "general", label: language.t("settings.tab.general") }, { value: "shortcuts", label: language.t("settings.tab.shortcuts") }, { value: "agents", label: language.t("settings.tab.agents") }, { value: "providers", label: language.t("settings.providers.title") }, { value: "models", label: language.t("settings.models.title") }, ] as const const visible = createMemo(() => { const q = search().toLowerCase() if (!q) return () => true return (tab: typeof TABS[number]) => tab.label.toLowerCase().includes(q) }) // ── Keyboard nav ── let listRef: HTMLDivElement | undefined const onListKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => { if (e.key !== "ArrowUp" && e.key !== "ArrowDown") return e.preventDefault() const triggers = Array.from( listRef?.querySelectorAll('[role="tab"]') ?? [] ) const idx = triggers.indexOf(document.activeElement as HTMLButtonElement) const delta = e.key === "ArrowDown" ? 1 : -1 const next = (idx + delta + triggers.length) % triggers.length triggers[next]?.focus() } return (
{/* Search input */}
setSearch(e.currentTarget.value)} />
{language.t("settings.section.desktop")}
{language.t("settings.tab.general")} {language.t("settings.tab.shortcuts")} {language.t("settings.tab.agents")}
{language.t("settings.section.server")}
{language.t("settings.providers.title")} {language.t("settings.models.title")}
{language.t("app.name.desktop")} v{platform.version}
) } ``` **Keyboard nav details:** - `onKeyDown` on container div inside Tabs.List - ArrowUp/Down: query `[role="tab"]` elements, find `document.activeElement`, cycle focus - No Tabs API knowledge needed — browser fires click/activation on Enter/Space when trigger is focused - Triggers hidden by `Show` are simply absent from DOM — not in querySelector result **Search details:** - `visible()` returns a filter function that checks `String.includes` on label text - When search is empty, all tabs pass filter - When search has text, only matching tab labels render their trigger - Active tab's trigger may be hidden, but it remains active and keyboard nav still works **Icon verification:** Verify `Icon name="bot"` exists. If not, use `Icon name="user"` or `Icon name="sliders"`. **2. packages/app/src/i18n/en.ts** (+2 keys) - `settings.search.placeholder`: "Search settings..." - `settings.tab.agents`: "Agents" **3. packages/app/src/i18n/*.ts** — add both keys to all locales --- ## Order of Implementation All three features are independent. Recommended order: 1. **Feature 2** — smallest change, immediate visual impact 2. **Feature 3** — unblocks Feature 1 by adding agents tab 3. **Feature 1** — largest implementation ## File Change Summary | File | Action | Feature | |------|--------|---------| | `packages/app/src/components/dialog-manage-agents.tsx` | CREATE | F1 | | `packages/app/src/components/dialog-edit-agent.tsx` | CREATE | F1 | | `packages/app/src/components/settings-agents.tsx` | CREATE | F1 | | `packages/app/src/components/dialog-settings.tsx` | MODIFY | F1+F3 | | `packages/app/src/i18n/en.ts` | MODIFY | F1+F3 | | `packages/app/src/i18n/*.ts` | MODIFY | F1+F3 | | `packages/app/src/components/session-context-usage.tsx` | MODIFY | F2 | ## Risk Assessment ### Feature 1 Risks | Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation | |------|-----------|------------| | localStorage unavailable in SSR/private mode | Low | try/catch in load/save; fallback empty array | | `Icon name="bot"` doesn't exist | Medium | Use `Icon name="user"` or `Icon name="sliders"` | | SDK agent list loads after dialog renders | Medium | createMemo handles reactivity; List re-renders | ### Feature 2 Risks | Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation | |------|-----------|------------| | CSS var not in all themes | Very Low | Verified: exists in v1 and v2 light+dark themes | | --border-active override affects other elements | Low | Style scoped to wrapper div via inline style | ### Feature 3 Risks | Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation | |------|-----------|------------| | Search hides active tab trigger | Medium | No-op: tab stays active, keyboard nav still works on hidden trigger | | Arrow key conflicts with TextField | Low | TextField handles its own keys; onKeyDown is on outer container | | TextField import not available | Very Low | Already available in @opencode-ai/ui/text-field | ## Verification Strategy ### Type Check ``` cd packages/app && bun typecheck ``` ### Feature 1 Verification 1. Settings → Agents tab visible between Shortcuts and Providers 2. "Manage agents" → dialog shows SDK agents 3. Create agent → name+prompt+model → save → list updates 4. Edit/delete custom agents 5. Refresh page → custom agents persist in localStorage 6. SDK agents show "Built-in" badge (read-only) ### Feature 2 Verification 1. Open session → context usage circle in timeline header 2. Circle color: default → warning → danger at thresholds 3. Sessions with compaction → compaction count badge visible 4. Compaction divider in timeline with "Compaction" label ### Feature 3 Verification 1. Settings → search input at tab list top 2. Type "gen" → only General visible; "mod" → only Models visible 3. Clear search → all tabs reappear 4. Arrow keys cycle through visible tabs (wrapping) 5. Agents tab visible and navigable ## Rollback Plan Revert commits in reverse build order: 1. `git revert ` — 3 new files + modifications 2. `git revert ` — dialog-settings + i18n 3. `git revert ` — session-context-usage only
revision_notes: Added compaction count badge to Feature 2 (using sync.data.part to count compaction parts). Fixed keyboard navigation approach to use querySelector('[role="tab"]') focus cycling instead of Tabs API. Simplified search filter to use a filter function pattern. Restructured dialog-settings pseudo-code to show full component for clarity.
